description: Which subagents and skills use prompt caching and what they cache. Caching reduces cost and latency for components that repeatedly read large stable contexts.

foreman-skill.applies_to: main-thread orchestration mode (foreman is a skill, not a subagent — see ADR-008)
foreman-skill.rationale: Main thread reads foreman skill + routing config + stack-config on every dispatch decision. These rarely change within a session.

foreman-team-lead.rationale: Team-lead session uses the foreman skill as routing source of truth + needs agent list for spawning teammates.

architecture-critic.rationale: Reads entire repo every invocation. Massive cache benefit.

historian.rationale: Reads all archived state monthly. Same input set across runs.

reviewer.rationale: Reads project conventions on every review.

security-auditor.rationale: Reads security posture + schema on every audit.

implementation_notes.anthropic: Use cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' } on cached messages. 5-minute TTL.
implementation_notes.openai: Use Prompt Caching API; cached prefixes auto-detected by repeated content >1024 tokens.
implementation_notes.google: Use context caching for Gemini 1.5+; explicit CachedContent objects.

monitoring: cache_read_tokens and cache_write_tokens are logged to cost_log table. Tier 4 /agent-performance-review reports cache hit rate.
